diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was made by CT scanning, which showed a deficiency in the interarytenoideus muscle and a defect in the lamina of the cricoid cartilage. The patient had a life-long history of a weak, breathy voice and stridor on strenuous exertion. Previous direct laryngoscopy had revealed a small lesion of the right aryepiglottic fold but was otherwise reported as normal. The CT scan findings were pivotal in confirming the diagnosis.
